THE GC10X FORMULA
[Video coming soon]
Here's my secret Facebook Page strategy I am calling the
GC10X FANPAGE FORMULA.
Goal: to reduce or keep CPMs low in your ad account by
increasing Page Post engagement.
How? Although Page engagement has been a thing for
some years, in 2018 I noticed a far bigger trend towards
high engagement pages receiving favourable CPMs in
their ad accounts.
These pages had:
- On average 10K fans (the more fans the bigger the
positive impact)
- On average 3 posts per week to the Page
- Regular post engagement in the form of likes and
comments in particular
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 5 --

- The majority of posts were NOT selling anything but
adding value in some form
So I wanted to amplify this and see if I could improve
Page engagement and drop CPMs. Did it work?
Yes, in spectacular fashion. Although it worked well on
ALL accounts the break out was our Ski clients in
November 2018 - whilst scaling to spending $341K in the
month to generate $7.1M in revenue (21X ROAS) we saw
CPMs of between $5-10 which is NOT normal for the
busiest time of the year for Ecom.
NEW PAGE STRATEGY
1. Launch 'posts' as ads (not to your page), test in PPE
with small spends, testing copy, creative, CTA etc. Ideally
if you have more than 2K fans, test against your fans
audience and friends of fans. Else test against those that
have engaged not clicked. If you have no fans or engaged
audiences (i.e new ad account, new page) then do some
broad targeting against your rough demographic
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

2. Track cost per Engagement and if running a video, cost
per TrueView - this is Facebook's measure of 15 seconds
of continuous viewing (better for videos 30seconds or
longer) or else cost per 10 second view. Also keep an eye
out for the level of comments engagement.
3. Push the 'ad' with the most engagement to your Page
as a new post. It'll start-off with zero social proof but since
you know it works, give it a boost minimum $10/d ideally
$50/d for a faster boost - start with fans, then friends of
fans, customers and engagers. Use a PPE campaign to
'boost' the post (don't use the Boost button in the Page as
this gives you less control).
4. If the post is good and relevant, organic will do the rest
EXISTING PAGE STRATEGY
1. If I have an existing Page with existing posts, I'd use
Facebook's Split Test feature to load up the ads against
my target audiences, drop around $100 per ad (so if
testing 3, $300) in a 5-day long test as above.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

2. Track cost per Engagement and if running a video, cost
per TrueView - this is Facebook's measure of 15 seconds
of continuous viewing (better for videos 30seconds or
longer) or else cost per 10 second view. Also keep an eye
out for the level of comments engagement.
3. Pause the worst performer(s) and continue pushing
spend to your winner(s). Depending on current CPMs you
can keep this ticking over for between $10 and $50
depending on overall budgets. If you're spending over
5-figures per day then I'd consider going more aggressive
at $200/d to promote the post
4. Cycle the ads and continue testing.
Notes:
- I prefer using high value videos in this strategy; not only
does it engage better, Facebook favours keeping people
on Facebook (as opposed to a blog link for example) AND
you can retarget the video view audience (% watched)
AND create a lookalike from prime audiences.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 5 --

Ultimately the goal here is to create a continuous motion
of high value organic content to build audiences, reduce
CPMs and enable your sales-focussed ad account to
benefit from higher page engagement scores and lower
CPMs.
If you're looking for a live example in motion this is
EXACTLY what I'm doing over on my page:
www.facebook.com/MrDepeshMandalia
I frequently promote high value content to my fans, FOF
and cold audiences to build up page value, create new
custom audiences and lookalikes as a way of reaching
more and more relevant people and keeping my costs
low.
As I write this in January 2019 my 21X Case Study
campaign is attracting CPMs of $3 (that's $3 to reach
1000 impressions) as my account and Page engagement
scores are so high.
Share your current CPM in the group and track back in a
few weeks to share your improvements!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
